executive_summary.key_insight: ControlPartner is a small but established Norwegian automation specialist with solid 2023 financials and strong technical capabilities. Recent Uniwater acquisition provides strategic backing but introduces integration uncertainty. Limited scale and single-year financial data constrain risk assessment confidence.
executive_summary.data_confidence: MEDIUM — Single year financials, limited public disclosure

recommendation.confidence: Medium confidence due to data limitations
conditions: Confirm Uniwater acquisition completion and integration plan
Obtain parent company guarantee for contracts >NOK 10M
Require key person retention clauses for critical personnel
Conduct site audit of Porsgrunn facilities before contract award
Establish clear escalation path to Uniwater management

profile.description: ControlPartner AS is a Norwegian specialist in operational control and automation systems, serving municipal water/wastewater and maritime sectors. Founded in 2015 (predecessor from 1996), the company provides complete automation systems with SCADA, operational control facilities, and electrical systems integration. Acquired by Swedish water infrastructure group Uniwater in October 2025.

market_position.market_context: Niche player in Norwegian water/wastewater automation
advantages: Deep expertise in Norwegian water infrastructure standards
Strong local service presence (Porsgrunn, Bergen, Oslo)
Now part of Uniwater group with access to capital

risks.description: Recent Uniwater acquisition creates integration uncertainty
risks.mitigation: Parent company guarantee; clear communication protocols

risks.description: Small scale limits loss absorption capacity
risks.mitigation: Parent company backing; phased payment terms

risks.description: Single-year financial data limits risk assessment confidence

timeline.milestone: Expansion to Bergen and Oslo offices

operational_capability.key_facilities: Headquarters Porsgrunn with offices in Bergen and Oslo

commercial_intelligence.peer_comparison: Above average on technical capability and service; competitive on price
negotiation_notes: Uniwater acquisition may create pricing pressure to win market share
Strong preference for long-term service contracts
Willing to negotiate on payment terms for strategic clients
Limited flexibility on hourly rates for specialists
Value-based pricing for complex automation projects
commercial_intelligence.payment_terms: Standard 30 days; negotiable for strategic relationships

assumptions: ISO 14001 assumed based on sector standards

full_product_1_not_possible: Limited peer financial data for comparison
lite_version_notes.recommendation: Proceed with caution. Request 2024 financials when available post-acquisition.
